subject: Project Management
title: Updating Your Beliefs
description: A quick win: understand Updating Your Beliefs.

hook.text: When new evidence arrives mid-project, do you update your plan — or defend your original estimate?

buildup.text: Most teams anchor to their first estimate. New information gets explained away to fit the existing plan.

discovery.text: Bayesian thinking says: start with a belief, then update it proportionally when new evidence arrives.

twist.text: Updating feels like admitting you were wrong. In practice, it's the most rational thing a PM can do.

climax.text: The best project managers change their minds. The worst ones defend their original plans to the grave.

punchline.text: Changing your mind isn't weakness. It's intelligence.

quiz.question: What does Bayesian thinking mean for project managers?
options: Update your estimates when new evidence arrives
Never change your plan once it's set
Trust your gut over the data
